text:
Market today: Tech is bouncing back after a global AI selloff hit chip names hard. The rebound is real but fragile — you're in a margin account, so a shaky recovery is not the same as being out of the woods.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 12.89%, largest position, and today's news that OpenAI is building chips with Broadcom is a double-edged sword (validates the business but signals customer independence long-term). PLTR is down 10.31% with no obvious catalyst to reverse it — watch closely. SPCX is a brand-new IPO with very limited data; it's already down 2.97% and sitting close to your $150 stop — on margin, a fast drop here hits hard. SNOW is your bright spot at +48% — make sure that $208 stop is live to protect that gain.

Watch: Whether today's tech rebound has follow-through volume, or fades into close. Also watch AVGO specifically around the OpenAI chip news — more detail could move it sharply either way.

Bottom line: Don't let a relief bounce distract you — AVGO and PLTR are bleeding on your largest bets and margin makes every dip cost real money.
